response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior web/full-stack engineer, but this role's hard requirements are almost entirely unmet: there is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (an explicit requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, no L2/L3 networking or switch experience, and no security-technology background (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems). C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, while his career has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms. The mismatch between his profile and an embedded LAN-switch security firmware role is fundamental, not marginal.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience, well above the 3-year minimum
Some low-level and systems exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and open-source contributions
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language ability, which is a stated hard requirement
No embedded networking, switch, security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel modification experience
Primary expertise is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far from firmware-level C development for UNIX

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
